They asked me to submit my documents through an online application link. After that, I was asked to come to their office to complete a required pre-employment test.
I completed the test and provided all the documents they requested for registration. After everything was done, I was not informed about any specific job, and they stopped responding to my calls and messages.
Every time I went to their office in person, they told me that there was no work available, without any clear explanation or follow-up, even though I had completed all the required steps.
This experience was extremely frustrating and unprofessional, and it wasted my time and effort with no result.
Based on my experience, I do not recommend this employment agency.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Fenil Soni","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIO1lZmmo33vBHMF8leX9rGcyaxqq3fi0yA1i-TCG4BidLcfQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Big thanks to Harpreet Grewal at Pivotal Staffing for helping me get a job. On the positive side, my counsellor was genuinely friendly and professional throughout our interactions, which I appreciated.

However, I was disappointed with the lack of helpful information provided. I found myself more aware of current market trends and developments in the employment services industry than my counsellor seemed to be. The guidance I received didn’t feel particularly tailored to my situation.

I understand it’s a challenging economy, and employment agencies face their own constraints. It’s possible they lack the resources or specialized skills needed to address certain employment barriers that some clients face.

Despite these concerns, I want to emphasize that my counsellor was really kind and made an effort to be supportive. I connected with Jodi from the Kitchener Robert Half team in July and it's now November - haven't been contacted once with a single job opportunity (part time, full time, contract, ect.). Not one. They provide no updates, you have to reach out and check in if anything has come up. It's been four months and to not have been sent ONE job opportunity is insane. Better off using Indeed, Glassdoor, LinkedIn or your own network than relying on Robert Half in Kitchener (or better yet, another employment agency).","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Mahipal Kaur","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXIx8cMljm2fq3yB-SeNf502ATeKkuh1PfVZVnEf88yjFvzEUDv=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Very disappointing experience. I registered with this consultancy 6 months ago, but there has been absolutely no support from their side. Earlier I was in brampton. I received a call from one of the recruiter but my profile didn't match what she was looking for, though she told me somebody would call me as per my experience although I haven't heard anything from them. Now I moved to Kitchener, again trying to get job with their assistance still no help. Reception picks the call direct it to the consultants but the consultants rarely pick up calls, and when they do, they are unhelpful. I was simply told to search and apply for jobs on their website myself and that if a company is interested, they would contact me directly. That defeats the entire purpose of a job consultancy. No guidance, no follow-ups, and no active assistance in finding a job. Would not recommend to job seekers expecting real support.

I have been trying through their website since so long, I am not getting anything that was the reason I called Robert half. I am in a real need that is why I am coming to you back and forth. user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Capt SK Navis","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVPt8umXAeUDxVzzHypaT1wUUNP5_GwjVwDzwFy_kGLDqupNj0Y=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We visited State & Main in Kitchener for the first time on Family Day for a family dinner, and it was a wonderful experience from start to finish.

To drink, we ordered the Tank Mill Street Haze Phaze, which was crisp, smooth, and very refreshing. The Passion Palmer Mocktail and Raspberry Dragon Fruit No-Jito were both beautifully presented and packed with flavor ..not overly sweet, just perfectly balanced and refreshing.

For our appetizer, we had the Chili Coconut Calamari, and it was excellent. The calamari was lightly battered and crispy without being greasy, and the chili coconut flavor added a unique sweet-and-spicy twist that really stood out. It was a great way to start the meal.

For mains, we ordered the Teriyaki Rice Bowl, Truffle & Shrimp Rigatoni, and the 8oz Sirloin. The Teriyaki Rice Bowl was flavorful and satisfying, with a nice balance of sauce and fresh ingredients. The Truffle & Shrimp Rigatoni was rich and creamy with perfectly cooked shrimp and a delicious truffle aroma that wasn’t overpowering. The 8oz Sirloin was cooked just right, tender, and well-seasoned.
The atmosphere was warm and inviting, making it a perfect spot for a family celebration. Even though it was a holiday and quite busy, the service was excellent. A special thank you to Emily, who was friendly, attentive, and made sure we had everything we needed throughout the evening.

Overall, we had a fantastic first visit and will definitely be returning. We ordered the Pretzel Bites, Baja Fish Tacos, The Main burger, and the Grilled Chicken Club.

The Pretzel Bites were a great start—soft, warm, and perfectly salted, paired with a delicious beer cheese dip that had just the right amount of tang. The Baja Fish Tacos were fresh and flavorful, with crispy battered fish, creamy avocado, and a crunchy slaw. They came with fries, which were tasty and paired well with the garlic aioli, though the portion of fries was on the smaller side.

The Main burger was a solid classic—two juicy patties, melty cheese, and a nice balance of toppings. The Grilled Chicken Club was another standout, especially with the addition of avocado and peppered bacon, which gave it a nice savory punch.

Service was friendly and attentive throughout, and the atmosphere was relaxed and welcoming. Our server was attentive and did quality checks throughout the meal. However, the biggest issue was the lack of communication around our reservation. I noted that we were celebrating a birthday when booking, but it was never acknowledged. The server didn’t ask if we were celebrating anything, which makes it awkward to bring up on your own. This also suggests that reservation notes aren’t being communicated from the host to the server.

Unfortunately, my nearly $40 steak had absolutely no seasoning, which was disappointing given the price point. When dessert was offered, again there was no mention of a birthday.

The bill came to nearly $200, and the tip options were automatically set to 18%, 20%, or 22%. For that cost and a $30+ tip, I expect better attention to detail—especially something as simple as acknowledging a celebration noted on the reservation. With better communication and more consistency in food quality, this could have been a much stronger experience.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Matthew McKenna","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXDe7p-uMDCh9WvQ_VL1xM4ifptzB-2jDwUkusRqoyrt1jSXwA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I haven't had a burger this good in a long time.
